scope_note: QA Synchronous Harmonics Ceiling cert [318]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1993) Quantum Arithmetic Book 2 (QA-2), ITAM Portland, ISBN 1-883401-07-0, Chapter 6 pp.84-99. Companion cert [147] covers wavelet sync at product and par interference rules. This cert covers the 5040 ceiling content from the final third of Ch.6. CLAIM: (C1) 5040=2^4*3^2*5*7, tau(5040)=60, extremal (max tau below 5040 = 48), divisible by all 1-10, 5039 prime, 5041=71^2 with 3 divisors. (C2) all 21 harmonic fractions {k/d*5040} for d in {2,3,4,5,6,7} and k in {1,...,d-1} are positive integers, range 720-4320 (the 'harmonic points of a wavelength of 5040 units' per Iverson p.85). (C3) minimum quantum wavelength with 5 distinct prime factors including 2 and 3 is 2310=2*3*5*7*11 (primorial of first 5 primes); with 7 factors is 510510=2*3*5*7*11*13*17; both divisible by 6. (C4) integers 5041-5045 are each not divisible by 6 (not quantum-eligible); 5046=2*3*29^2 has only 3 distinct prime factors (below minimum 5). (C5) Theorem NT: continuous frequency spectra and spectral lines are observer projections; 5040 harmonic fractions, tau=60, and minimum QW wavelengths are discrete QA claims; Samekh (Iverson's hypothetical number violating unique factorization, p.83) is explicitly open per primary source. Builds on cert [147] (synchronous harmonics wavelet sync + par rules).
